response:
# Triage plan — top 10 findings, `01-messy-ts-app`

Ordered by `agent_risk` (the differentiator per the PRD), then grouped by detector category. Adding **testability/temporal** as a sixth bucket — five of the top ten are date-handling findings clustered in `src/date.ts` and `src/billing.ts`, and folding them into "structural" would hide that signal. No git-history findings (all `churn: 0`).

## Testability / temporal — **fix now, one PR**
All four live in `src/date.ts` and share root causes (no injected clock, no enforced timezone discipline). Doing them piecemeal will re-touch the same 50 lines four times.

| # | Charge | id | Why this PR |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Temporal Recklessness (`date.ts`, 8×) | `crime_00002` | Inject a `now()` clock; tests stop depending on wall time. |
| 2 | Half-UTC, Half-Local (`d` mixes UTC/local) | `crime_00003` | Pick UTC for the receiver; one-line fix once clock is in. |
| 5 | Timezone Roulette (unsafe `new Date("…")`) | `crime_00007` | Append `Z` to the two literals (L29, L33). |
| 7 | DST-Naive Day Math (`+ 86400000`) | `crime_00011` | Swap for Luxon/Temporal `addDays`. |

Plus the related billing.ts copy (`crime_00006`, just outside top 10) folds into the same clock-injection commit.

## Structural — **fix now, separate PR**
| # | Charge | id | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3 | God Function `generateInvoice` (204 LOC) | `crime_00001` | Extract by responsibility. Unblocks `crime_00013` (large_file) and most of `crime_00025` (todo density in same file) for free. |

## IA / agent-context — **fix now, cheapest win**
| # | Charge | id | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4 | Missing Agent Context (no `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md`) | `crime_00004` | Highest ROI of the lot — a 30-minute `AGENTS.md` flips agent_risk on every future scan. Do this first. |

## IA / frontend (route + nav drift) — **fix now, bundle together**
Both point at the same destination `/settings/billing` with three competing names ("Plans" / "Billing" / "Subscription"). One canonical-name decision fixes both.

| # | Charge | id | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 9 | Duplicated Navigation Source | `crime_00015` | Same root cause as #10 — fix together. |
| 10 | Route Metadata Drift | `crime_00017` | Pick one label; align route file, `metadata.title`, `<title>`, both nav files. |

## Duplication — **fix now**
| # | Charge | id | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | String Sprinkles — `"enterprise"` in 4 files | `crime_00009` | Centralize as a `Plan` enum or constants module. Pair with `crime_00010` (False Identity — `calculateRefundPreview` does side effects) since both live in `billing.ts` and the comment-rule (`crime_00014`) about "only owners can refund enterprise plans" should land as a typed guard at the same time. |

## IA / concept drift — **defer**
| # | Charge | id | Why defer |
|---|---|---|---|
| 8 | Concept Alias Drift — `tenant`/`account`/`organisation`/`team`/`workspace` | `crime_00012` | Don't refactor this mechanically. Needs product/design to pick the canonical noun; doing it from the code alone risks shipping the *wrong* canonical term and rewriting twice. Park until naming is decided. |

## Recommended PR order
1. **PR-1**: Add `AGENTS.md` (`crime_00004`) — minutes, biggest agent-risk drop, makes every later agent edit safer.
2. **PR-2**: `date.ts` clock + UTC + parse + DST cleanup (`crime_00002`, `_00003`, `_00006`, `_00007`, `_00011`).
3. **PR-3**: Extract `generateInvoice` (`crime_00001`) — also lets you centralize `"enterprise"` (`crime_00009`) and encode the comment rule (`crime_00014`) in the same touch.
4. **PR-4**: `/settings/billing` naming reconciliation (`crime_00015` + `crime_00017`).
5. **Backlog**: `crime_00012` (alias drift) — needs naming decision.

## Note on `crimes verdict`
The scenario referenced `crimes verdict --format json` but only `scan` output was provided. `verdict` would normally give the aggregate risk roll-up I'd use to confirm this ordering at the repo level; the per-finding `agent_risk` here is a reasonable proxy.
